Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Tmux Sessionizer with Default Sessions

## Context
The dotfiles already have 4 named tmux sessions managed individually via fish functions:
- `tpo` → `primary`, `tmo` → `mobile`, `tdo` → `desktop`, `two` → `work`

The goal is a **sessionizer** — an fzf-powered picker that lists default sessions, creates them if missing, and switches to them. This replaces manually typing `tpo`/`tmo` etc. and ensures default sessions always exist.

Existing relevant pieces:
- `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_t{po,mo,do,wo}_function.fish` — individual session attach fns
- `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` — shellAbbrs + xdg.configFile function list
- `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` — tmux keybinds, `tmux-sessionx` bound to `o`
- `tmux-resurrect` + `tmux-continuum` already configured for session persistence

## Implementation

### 1. New fish function: `_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish`

`home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish`

```fish
function _tmux_sessionizer_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then existing sessions not in default list
  set existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)
  test -z "$selected" && return

  if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
    end
  else
    tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
    end
  end
end
```

### 2. Register abbreviation in `default.nix`

Add to `shellAbbrs`:
```nix
ts = "_tmux_sessionizer_function";
```

Add to the `xdg.configFile` function list:
```nix
"_tmux_sessionizer_function"
```

### 3. Bind to tmux key in `tmux.conf`

Add a keybind so it's accessible from inside tmux (replaces or complements `tmux-sessionx`):
```
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tmux_sessionizer_function'"
```

This opens the sessionizer in a temporary window, switches session, then closes.

## Files to Modify
- `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish` — **create**
- `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` — add abbr `tss` + register function
- `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` — add `bind S` keybind

## Verification
1. Run `tss` in fish shell → fzf list shows `primary mobile desktop work` + any existing sessions
2. Select a session that doesn't exist → it gets created and attached
3. Select an existing session → switches to it
4. Inside tmux: `prefix + S` → same picker opens in a new window
5. All existing `tpo`/`tmo`/`tdo`/`two` abbreviations continue working unchanged

# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

function _fzf_git_branch --description="fzf git branch picker"
    # Get all branches sorted by most recent commit, remove leading whitespace and asterisk, deduplicate
    set -l branches (git branch -a --sort=-committerdate 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^[ *]*//' | sed 's|remotes/origin/||' | grep -v '^HEAD' | awk '!seen[$0]++')

    if test -z "$branches"
        echo "No branches found (not a git repository?)"
        return 1
    end

    set -l selected_branch (printf '%s\n' $branches | fzf --prompt=(_fzf_preview_name "Branch") --preview='echo "Last commit: $(git log -1 --format="%cr" {} 2>/dev/null)"; echo ""; git log --oneline --color=always -n 10 {}' --preview-window right:50%)

    if test -n "$selected_branch"
        git checkout $selected_branch
    end

    commandline --function repaint
end

function _fzf_ghq_picker --description="fzf ghq repository picker"
    set -l selected_repo (ghq list | fzf --prompt=(_fzf_preview_name "Repository") --preview 'ghq look {} --dry-run | head -1' --preview-window right:30%)

    end
    
    commandline --function repaint
end

function _tmux_sessionizer_function --description="Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
    set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

    # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then any extra existing sessions
    set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
    set -l candidates $default_sessions
    for s in $existing
        if not contains $s $default_sessions
            set -a candidates $s
        end
    end

    set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

    if test -n "$selected"
        if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
            if test -n "$TMUX"
                tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
            else
                tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
            end
        else
            tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
            if test -n "$TMUX"
                tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
            else
                tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
            end
        end
    end
end

function _tmux_sessionizer_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then any extra existing sessions
  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end

# Plan: Tmux Sessionizer with Default Sessions

## Context
The dotfiles already have 4 named tmux sessions managed individually via fish functions:
- `tpo` → `primary`, `tmo` → `mobile`, `tdo` → `desktop`, `two` → `work`

The goal is a **sessionizer** — an fzf-powered picker that lists default sessions, creates them if missing, and switches to them. This replaces manually typing `tpo`/`tmo` etc. and ensures default sessions always exist.

Existing relevant pieces:
- `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_t{po,mo,do,wo}_function.fish` — individual session attach fns
- `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` — shellAbbrs + xdg.configFile function list
- `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` — tmux keybinds, `tmux-sessionx` bound to `o`
- `tmux-resurrect` + `tmux-continuum` already configured for session persistence

## Implementation

### 1. New fish function: `_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish`

`home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish`

```fish
function _tmux_sessionizer_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then existing sessions not in default list
  set existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)
  test -z "$selected" && return

  if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
    end
  else
    tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
    end
  end
end
```

### 2. Register abbreviation in `default.nix`

Add to `shellAbbrs`:
```nix
ts = "_tmux_sessionizer_function";
```

Add to the `xdg.configFile` function list:
```nix
"_tmux_sessionizer_function"
```

### 3. Bind to tmux key in `tmux.conf`

Add a keybind so it's accessible from inside tmux (replaces or complements `tmux-sessionx`):
```
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tmux_sessionizer_function'"
```

This opens the sessionizer in a temporary window, switches session, then closes.

## Files to Modify
- `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish` — **create**
- `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` — add abbr `tss` + register function
- `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` — add `bind S` keybind

## Verification
1. Run `tss` in fish shell → fzf list shows `primary mobile desktop work` + any existing sessions
2. Select a session that doesn't exist → it gets created and attached
3. Select an existing session → switches to it
4. Inside tmux: `prefix + S` → same picker opens in a new window
5. All existing `tpo`/`tmo`/`tdo`/`two` abbreviations continue working unchanged

# Plan: Tmux Session Presets via tmuxinator

## Context
The 4 named sessions (`primary`, `mobile`, `desktop`, `work`) are created bare with `tmux new-session -A -s <name>`. The goal is to have each session automatically start with predefined windows when first created (editor, shell, server, etc.). **tmuxinator** is the chosen mechanism — it reads a YAML layout file per session and wires up windows/panes on first create, then falls back to normal attach on subsequent invocations.

## Implementation

### 1. Add `pkgs.tmuxinator` in `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`

```nix
{ pkgs, ... }:
{
  home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];

  programs.tmux = { ... };  # unchanged
}
```

### 2. Create YAML layout files (one per session)

Stored in `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmuxinator/` and registered via `xdg.configFile` in `default.nix`:

```nix
xdg.configFile = {
  "tmuxinator/primary.yml".source = ./tmuxinator/primary.yml;
  "tmuxinator/mobile.yml".source  = ./tmuxinator/mobile.yml;
  "tmuxinator/desktop.yml".source = ./tmuxinator/desktop.yml;
  "tmuxinator/work.yml".source    = ./tmuxinator/work.yml;
};
```

**`primary.yml`** — editor + shell + server:
```yaml
name: primary
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`mobile.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: mobile
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`desktop.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: desktop
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`work.yml`** — editor + shell:
```yaml
name: work
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
```

### 3. Update the 4 individual fish functions

Pattern: if session exists → attach/switch normally; if new → `tmuxinator start <name>` (creates layout and attaches/switches automatically, respecting `$TMUX`).

**`_tpo_function.fish`**:
```fish
function _tpo_function --description "Attach to tmux primary session"
  if tmux has-session -t primary 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t primary
    else
      tmux attach-session -t primary
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start primary
  end
end
```

Same pattern for `_tmo_function` (`mobile`), `_tdo_function` (`desktop`), `_two_function` (`work`).

### 4. Update `_tss_function.fish` (sessionizer)

For default sessions use tmuxinator; for ad-hoc sessions fall back to plain `tmux new-session`:

```fish
function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end
```

## Files to Modify/Create
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Add `home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ]` + `xdg.configFile` entries |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmuxinator/primary.yml` | **create** |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmuxinator/mobile.yml` | **create** |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmuxinator/desktop.yml` | **create** |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmuxinator/work.yml` | **create** |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tpo_function.fish` | Update to use tmuxinator |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmo_function.fish` | Update to use tmuxinator |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tdo_function.fish` | Update to use tmuxinator |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish` | Update to use tmuxinator |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Update to use tmuxinator for defaults |

## Verification
1. After `home-manager switch`, run `tpo` with no existing `primary` session → tmuxinator creates it with 3 windows (editor, shell, server)
2. Run `tpo` again → attaches normally without re-applying layout
3. Run `tss`, select `mobile` (non-existent) → tmuxinator creates it with 2 windows
4. Run `tss`, select an existing session → switch/attach as before
5. Run `tss`, type a new ad-hoc name → plain `tmux new-session` (no YAML needed)

# Plan: Tmux Sessionizer with Default Sessions

## Context
The dotfiles already have 4 named tmux sessions managed individually via fish functions:
- `tpo` → `primary`, `tmo` → `mobile`, `tdo` → `desktop`, `two` → `work`

The goal is a **sessionizer** — an fzf-powered picker that lists default sessions, creates them if missing, and switches to them. This replaces manually typing `tpo`/`tmo` etc. and ensures default sessions always exist.

Existing relevant pieces:
- `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_t{po,mo,do,wo}_function.fish` — individual session attach fns
- `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` — shellAbbrs + xdg.configFile function list
- `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` — tmux keybinds, `tmux-sessionx` bound to `o`
- `tmux-resurrect` + `tmux-continuum` already configured for session persistence

## Implementation

### 1. New fish function: `_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish`

`home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish`

```fish
function _tmux_sessionizer_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then existing sessions not in default list
  set existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)
  test -z "$selected" && return

  if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
    end
  else
    tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
    end
  end
end
```

### 2. Register abbreviation in `default.nix`

Add to `shellAbbrs`:
```nix
ts = "_tmux_sessionizer_function";
```

Add to the `xdg.configFile` function list:
```nix
"_tmux_sessionizer_function"
```

### 3. Bind to tmux key in `tmux.conf`

Add a keybind so it's accessible from inside tmux (replaces or complements `tmux-sessionx`):
```
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tmux_sessionizer_function'"
```

This opens the sessionizer in a temporary window, switches session, then closes.

## Files to Modify
- `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish` — **create**
- `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` — add abbr `tss` + register function
- `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` — add `bind S` keybind

## Verification
1. Run `tss` in fish shell → fzf list shows `primary mobile desktop work` + any existing sessions
2. Select a session that doesn't exist → it gets created and attached
3. Select an existing session → switches to it
4. Inside tmux: `prefix + S` → same picker opens in a new window
5. All existing `tpo`/`tmo`/`tdo`/`two` abbreviations continue working unchanged

# Plan: Tmux Session Presets via tmuxinator

## Context
The 4 named sessions (`primary`, `mobile`, `desktop`, `work`) are created bare with `tmux new-session -A -s <name>`. The goal is to have each session automatically start with predefined windows when first created (editor, shell, server, etc.). **tmuxinator** is the chosen mechanism — it reads a YAML layout file per session and wires up windows/panes on first create, then falls back to normal attach on subsequent invocations.

## Implementation

### 1. Add `pkgs.tmuxinator` in `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`

```nix
{ pkgs, ... }:
{
  home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];

  programs.tmux = { ... };  # unchanged
}
```

### 2. Create YAML layout files (one per session)

Stored in `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmuxinator/` and registered via `xdg.configFile` in `default.nix`:

```nix
xdg.configFile = {
  "tmuxinator/primary.yml".source = ./tmuxinator/primary.yml;
  "tmuxinator/mobile.yml".source  = ./tmuxinator/mobile.yml;
  "tmuxinator/desktop.yml".source = ./tmuxinator/desktop.yml;
  "tmuxinator/work.yml".source    = ./tmuxinator/work.yml;
};
```

**`primary.yml`** — editor + shell + server:
```yaml
name: primary
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`mobile.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: mobile
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`desktop.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: desktop
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`work.yml`** — editor + shell:
```yaml
name: work
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
```

### 3. Update the 4 individual fish functions

Pattern: if session exists → attach/switch normally; if new → `tmuxinator start <name>` (creates layout and attaches/switches automatically, respecting `$TMUX`).

**`_tpo_function.fish`**:
```fish
function _tpo_function --description "Attach to tmux primary session"
  if tmux has-session -t primary 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t primary
    else
      tmux attach-session -t primary
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start primary
  end
end
```

Same pattern for `_tmo_function` (`mobile`), `_tdo_function` (`desktop`), `_two_function` (`work`).

### 4. Update `_tss_function.fish` (sessionizer)

For default sessions use tmuxinator; for ad-hoc sessions fall back to plain `tmux new-session`:

```fish
function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end
```

# Plan: Tmux Session Presets via tmuxinator

## Context
The 4 named sessions (`primary`, `mobile`, `desktop`, `work`) are created bare with `tmux new-session -A -s <name>`. The goal is to have each session automatically start with predefined windows when first created (editor, shell, server, etc.). **tmuxinator** is the chosen mechanism — it reads a YAML layout file per session and wires up windows/panes on first create, then falls back to normal attach on subsequent invocations.

Pattern from the repo:
- `config/` = XDG config file management (e.g. `config/jj/`, `config/ghostty/`)
- `home-manager/programs/` = package + program option configuration (e.g. `home-manager/programs/tms/`)

## Implementation

### 1. Install package in `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`

Add `home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];` alongside the existing `programs.tmux`:

```nix
{ pkgs, ... }:
{
  home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];

  programs.tmux = {
    enable = true;
    # ... unchanged
  };
}
```

### 2. Create `config/tmuxinator/` (new config directory)

**`config/tmuxinator/default.nix`** — following `config/jj/default.nix` pattern:
```nix
{ ... }:
{
  xdg.configFile."tmuxinator".source = ./tmuxinator;
}
```

Register in **`config/default.nix`** (alongside other entries):
```nix
./tmuxinator
```

### 3. Create YAML layout files in `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/`

(Mirrors `~/.config/tmuxinator/` under XDG)

**`primary.yml`** — editor + shell + server:
```yaml
name: primary
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`mobile.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: mobile
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`desktop.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: desktop
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`work.yml`** — editor + shell:
```yaml
name: work
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
```

### 4. Update the 4 individual fish functions

Pattern: if session exists → attach/switch normally; if new → `tmuxinator start <name>` (tmuxinator handles attach/switch-client automatically by detecting `$TMUX`).

**`_tpo_function.fish`**:
```fish
function _tpo_function --description "Attach to tmux primary session"
  if tmux has-session -t primary 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t primary
    else
      tmux attach-session -t primary
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start primary
  end
end
```

Same pattern for `_tmo_function` (`mobile`), `_tdo_function` (`desktop`), `_two_function` (`work`).

### 5. Update `_tss_function.fish` (sessionizer)

Default sessions use tmuxinator on first create; ad-hoc sessions use plain `tmux new-session`:

```fish
function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end
```

## Files to Modify/Create
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Add `home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ]` |
| `config/default.nix` | Add `./tmuxinator` entry |
| `config/tmuxinator/default.nix` | **create** — `xdg.configFile."tmuxinator".source = ./tmuxinator` |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/primary.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/mobile.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/desktop.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/work.yml` | **create** |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tpo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tdo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator for default sessions |

## Verification
1. After `home-manager switch`, run `tpo` with no existing `primary` session → tmuxinator creates it with 3 windows (editor, shell, server)
2. Run `tpo` again → attaches/switches normally without re-applying layout
3. Run `tss`, select `mobile` (non-existent) → tmuxinator creates it with 2 windows
4. Run `tss`, select an existing session → switch/attach as before
5. Run `tss`, type a new ad-hoc name → plain `tmux new-session` (no YAML needed)
